text: Two vessels A and B contain oxygen. The volume of B is twice that of A, the pressure of B is thrice that of A and the temperature of B is half of that of A. Then, find the ratio of number of molecules of oxygen in vessels A and B
explanation: From ideal gas equation, \(N_B / N_A = (p_B V_B T_A) / (p_A V_A T_B)\). Substituting the given ratios \(V_B = 2V_A\), \(p_B = 3p_A\), \(T_B = T_A / 2\) gives \(N_A : N_B = 1 : 12\).
name: Questions Item 1

text: The ratio of the adiabatic to isothermal elasticities of a triatomic (non-linear) gas is
explanation: For triatomic non-linear gas, degrees of freedom \(f = 6\), \(C_v = 3R\), \(C_p = 4R\). Thus \(\gamma = C_p / C_v = 4/3\). Ratio of adiabatic to isothermal elasticity is \(\gamma = 4:3\).
name: Questions Item 2

text: The average kinetic energy of H₂ molecules at 300 K is E. At the same temperature the average kinetic energy of O₂ molecule is
explanation: Average kinetic energy per molecule \(\frac{3}{2} kT\) depends only on temperature. Hence, it is same for both H₂ and O₂ at 300 K, equal to E.
name: Questions Item 3

text: A vessel contains mixture of hydrogen and oxygen gases in the ratio of their masses equal to 1 : 5. The ratio of mean kinetic energies of the two gases is
explanation: Mean kinetic energy per molecule \(\frac{3}{2} kT\) is same for both gases at same temperature. Hence ratio is 1 : 1.
name: Questions Item 4

text: Pressure of a gas of constant volume at 20°C is 90 cm of Hg. At what temperature the pressure would change to 75 cm of Hg?
explanation: At constant volume, \(p \propto T\). \(T_2 = T_1 \times (75/90) = 293 \times (75/90) = 244.16\) K = -28.84°C.
name: Questions Item 5

text: A light container having a diatomic gas enclosed within is moving with velocity v. Mass of the gas is M and number of moles is n. The kinetic energy of gas w.r.t. ground is
explanation: Total KE w.r.t ground = KE of centre of mass + internal KE. Centre of mass KE = \(\frac{1}{2} M v^2\). For diatomic gas internal KE = \(\frac{5}{2} nRT\). Hence total \(\frac{1}{2} M v^2 + \frac{5}{2} nRT\).
name: Questions Item 6

explanation: Average KE = \(\frac{3}{2} kT\). KE minimum (zero) at absolute zero temperature 0 K = -273 °C.
name: Questions Item 7

text: The average translational kinetic energy of a molecule in a gas becomes equal to 0.69 eV at temperature about
name: Questions Item 8

text: If the speed of sound in a mixture of 2 moles of Helium and 2 moles of Hydrogen at temperature 972/5 K is n × 100 ms⁻¹, then the value of n is
explanation: Effective molecular mass and γ of mixture calculated. Speed of sound gives 900 ms⁻¹, so n = 9.
name: Questions Item 9

text: For a gas the value of R/Cᵥ = 0.4, so the gas is
explanation: \(\gamma = 1 + R/C_v = 1.4\). This value of γ corresponds to diatomic gas.
name: Questions Item 10

text: Under standard conditions, the density of a gas is 1400/1089 kg m⁻³ and the speed of sound propagation in it is 330 m s⁻¹, then the number of degrees of freedom of the gas molecules is
explanation: \(\gamma = v^2 \rho / p = 1.4\). Hence degrees of freedom f = 5.
name: Questions Item 11

text: The y-components of velocities of the molecules of a gas are -7, -6, ..., +7 ms⁻¹ then the rms velocity is
explanation: rms = √(sum v_y² / 15). Sum of squares gives 280/2 = 140 for pairs, total rms = √(56/3).
name: Questions Item 12

text: The specific heat capacities of an ideal gas at constant pressure and at constant volume are 620 J kg⁻¹ K⁻¹ and 420 J kg⁻¹ K⁻¹ respectively. The density of the gas at STP is approximately
explanation: C_p - C_v = R/m gives molar mass m. Then density ρ = pM / RT ≈ 1.86 kg m⁻³.
name: Questions Item 13

text: Three closed vessels A, B and C are at the same temperature T and contain gases. Vessel A contains only O₂, B contains only N₂ and C contains a mixture of equal quantities of O₂ and N₂. If the rms speed of O₂ molecules in vessel A is v₁ and that of N₂ molecules in vessel B is v₂ then the rms speed of O₂ molecules in vessel C is
explanation: rms speed depends only on temperature and molar mass. In mixture, rms speed of O₂ remains v₁ (same T and M).
name: Questions Item 14

text: A thermally insulated vessel with nitrogen gas at 27°C is moving with a velocity of 100 ms⁻¹. If the vessel is stopped suddenly, then the percentage change in the pressure of the gas is nearly
explanation: KE loss converted to heat. Δp/p × 100 = M v² / (f R T) × 100 = 2.25%.
name: Questions Item 15

text: For a molecule of an ideal gas, the number density is 2√2 × 10⁸ cm⁻³ and the mean free path is 10^{-2}/π cm. The diameter of the gas molecule is
explanation: Mean free path λ = 1/(√2 π n d²). Solving gives d = 5 × 10^{-4} cm.
name: Questions Item 16

text: The absolute temperature at which the rms speed of a hydrogen molecule is equal to its escape speed from the moon's surface is
explanation: v_rms = √(3kT/m), v_escape = √(2gR). Set equal gives T = 2mgR / 3k.
name: Questions Item 17

text: A diatomic gas consisting of rigid molecules is at a temperature of 87°C. If the moment of inertia of the rotating diatomic rigid molecule is 2.76 × 10^{-39} g cm², then the rms angular speed of the molecule is
explanation: ω_rms = √(2kT / I). Substituting values gives 6 × 10^{12} rad s^{-1}.
name: Questions Item 18
